SECTION 2 — DEBT CEILING (DEBT/PLAFOND RATIO)
The Debt/Plafond ratio represents total outstanding debt as a proportion of the borrower's
approved credit plafond or total available credit.

Policy rule 2.1: Debt/Plafond ratio above 75% triggers mandatory manual review regardless

INTERNAL CREDIT POLICY v3.2
Document ID: ICP-3.2
Last reviewed: 2024-01

SECTION 1 — DEBT SERVICE TO INCOME (DSTI)
The DSTI ratio measures the proportion of a borrower's gross monthly income consumed by
total debt repayment obligations including the requested loan installment.

Policy rule 1.1: DSTI must not exceed 40% for standard personal loan and overdraft products.

Rule 1.1: Where DSTI exceeds 35% AND the annualised default probability is above 4%,
the application requires enhanced affordability assessment. The analyst must document
evidence that the borrower can sustain the obligation under stress conditions.

llm_output.rationale: The DSTI ratio exceeds the standard threshold of 40%, and the Debt/Plafond ratio is above the mandatory review threshold. According to internal_credit_policy, rule 1.2, this requires additional scrutiny.
llm_output.gdpr_art22_explanation: We have assessed your creditworthiness based on our internal policies and guidelines. Our decision takes into account various factors, including your debt-to-income ratio and the requested loan amount. If you wish to dispute any aspect of our assessment, please contact us.
